1.title: Jude, brother of James
1.body: The author introduces himself as “the servant of Jesus Christ, and brother of James”—traditionally identified as a brother of the Lord. His one-chapter letter urges believers to “contend for the faith.”

1.template: earnestly contend for the {0} which was once delivered unto the saints

1.question: Which cities does Jude cite as an example, “suffering the vengeance of eternal fire” (v. 7)?

1.title: Michael and the body of Moses
1.body: Jude alone in the Bible records that Michael the archangel “disputed about the body of Moses” with the devil, saying only, “The Lord rebuke thee.”

pairs.right: whose error they ran after for reward

1.template: wandering {0}, to whom is reserved the blackness of darkness

1.title: The prophecy of Enoch
1.body: Jude is the only New Testament book to quote a prophecy of Enoch, “the seventh from Adam,” foretelling that the Lord would come “with ten thousands of his saints” to execute judgment.

1.question: According to verse 14, who “prophesied” of these ungodly men, being “the seventh from Adam”?

1.template: Keep yourselves in the {0} of God, looking for the mercy of our Lord Jesus Christ
